text:
is a radio program and podcast commemorating the 30th anniversary of Neon Genesis Evangelion. It was broadcasted on 38 JFN stations, including TOKYO FM, every Saturday in the timeslot 22:30.

Overview

The program will feature creators, singers, and voice actors with a connection to the series, who will share unknown behind-the-scenes stories about the production, as well as artists who are huge fans of the series, who will talk about their own experiences with EVANGELION.

FM EVA 30.0 is also available as a podcast on . A special edited version including parts that couldn't be broadcast due to time constraints is available exclusively on Amazon Music!

Episodes

Episode 1
 
Yoko Takahashi - Memories engraved in song

"FM EVA 30.0," a special program celebrating the 30th anniversary of the EVANGELION series, has begun! The commemorative first episode features Yoko Takahashi, who sang the TV series' theme song, "Zankoku na Tenshi no These". Takahashi will personally share behind-the-scenes stories about the production, the appeal of the song, the atmosphere at the time of broadcast, and more, all of which are exclusive! Enjoy!

Episode 2-1
 
Fashion - Tomohiro Kato (Creative Director of "RADIO EVA") x Sekai (EXILE / FANTASTICS)

The world of EXILE/FANTASTICS has its first conversation with Tomohiro Kato, creative director of the official Evangelion apparel brand "RADIO EVA"! In the first part, they'll share their passionate stories of how they first encountered the series and their favorite scenes.

Episode 2-2

Fashion (Part 2) Tomohiro Kato (Creative Director of "RADIO EVA") x Sekai (EXILE / FANTASTICS)

The world of EXILE/FANTASTICS has its first conversation with Tomohiro Kato, creative director of the official Evangelion apparel brand "RADIO EVA"! In the second part, we'll be discussing a plethora of intriguing topics, including the thoughts on EVA that went into the apparel designs and the mysterious relationship between EVA and dance. Enjoy this 100% synchronized conversation.

Episode 3

EVA and Rakugo by Hayashiya Kikuhiko

Rakugo performer Hayashiya Kikuhiko, a big fan of Evangelion, talks about the appeal of the series. He'll discuss what it would be like to portray the characters in rakugo, and even announce the unveiling of "Evangelion Rakugo"! Enjoy the crossover between traditional performing arts and Evangelion.
